halt_rule: Any candidate has slope -> 0 at dps >= 3000 -> escalate to formal barrier-stratum paper.
summary: All 5 escalated candidates remain NULL across the 5-tier basis at dps=3000 with mu_res_slope = 0 and weak-PSLQ coef-growth = 0 (no integer relation found at any precision in [500, 1000, 1500, 3000]). The slope-to-zero criterion is satisfied for every escalated candidate.
basis_used: 5-tier: T1 (7) -> T2 (15) -> T3 (20) -> T4 (24) -> T5 (29). Includes pi, log {2,3,5}, zeta {3,5}, Catalan, Li_n(1/2), L(2,chi_-3), Gamma(1/3),(1/4),(1/6) and squares, AGM(1,sqrt2), omega_lem, e, exp(pi)-1, plus bilinear products.
caveats: Basis omits modular forms (Eisenstein E_4, E_6), theta values, hypergeometric values 2F1(a,b;c;x) at non-trivial x, Chowla-Selberg periods of CM elliptic curves, and L-values of weight >= 3.
Therefore 'NULL across this basis' is a NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT signature of T3.

The HALT trigger means: the basis-exhaustion test has not been able to demote these 5 within the resources budgeted; they are PROVISIONAL T3 candidates suitable for formal barrier-stratum analysis (NOT proven T3).
Sampling: 30 trans_hard examples loaded from T2A-DEGREE42-DEEP-VALIDATE concentrate in just 2 a-shapes ({1,-1,-1,-1,-1} and {1,-1,-1,-1,0}); the full 1162-family pool was not re-loaded this session.
next_action: Escalate to formal barrier-stratum paper. Recommended: build a Frobenius-method ODE for the 5 candidates' generating functions at the relevant CMAX=1 stratum, compute monodromy at all punctures, search for Heun / Painleve / 2F1-period closed forms outside the elementary basis.
